skill.name: agentdb-vector-search
skill.description: Build semantic vector search with AgentDB for document retrieval, similarity search, metadata filtering, and RAG workflows.
skill.summary: Use AgentDB to build fast vector search, hybrid search, and RAG retrieval workflows.
skill.author: ruvnet

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution findings are confirmed as user-facing shell examples, mainly npx agentdb@latest commands and MCP setup instructions. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md, but unpinned package execution and local vector database handling require user warnings.

medium_findings.title: Unpinned npx Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill repeatedly instructs users or agents to run npx agentdb@latest. This downloads and executes the latest package from npm, which can change over time and increases supply-chain risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain direct npx agentdb@latest commands. The commands are documented examples, not hidden execution, so the risk is real but context-dependent.

medium_findings.title: Local Vector Database and Export Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill creates, queries, exports, and imports local vector database files. These files may contain proprietary documents, embeddings, or metadata if users run the examples on sensitive data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly uses local database and backup files. This is legitimate functionality, but it creates data handling risk when documents are sensitive.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The skill links to a GitHub repository and project website. These links are normal documentation references and do not show data exfiltration or automatic network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain documentation URLs. No request code, credential transfer, or automatic fetch behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak-Cryptography Static Signal
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography, but the cited content describes semantic matching and vector quantization. No cryptographic algorithm use was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows vector-search wording, not hashing, encryption, or signature verification. This is a strong false positive.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Prompt-Injection and Credential-Access Heuristic
low_findings.description: The scanner reported a dangerous combination, but SKILL.md contains tutorial commands and code examples. No override instructions, credential theft, or hidden exfiltration text was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Broad review found no prompt-injection phrases or credential access instructions. Confidence is high because the only file is documentation, but runtime package behavior is outside this file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Version-Floating Package Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Commands use npx agentdb@latest instead of a pinned version. Users should pin and verify the package before running these commands in trusted workspaces.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact @latest command pattern appears in multiple cited examples. The risk depends on whether the user executes the commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: MCP Server Registration Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill shows a Claude Code MCP registration command that would add AgentDB as a local tool server. Users should review tool permissions before enabling it.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines explicitly start and register an MCP server. This is expected for the skill, but it expands local tool access.

content.value_statement: Semantic search setup can be complex when storing embeddings, filtering metadata, and tuning relevance. This skill provides AgentDB commands and patterns for retrieval and RAG workflows.
actual_capabilities: Shows AgentDB CLI commands for initializing, querying, exporting, importing, and inspecting vector databases.
Demonstrates TypeScript examples for storing documents with embeddings and metadata.
Explains similarity search, hybrid vector and metadata search, and threshold tuning.
Provides RAG retrieval patterns with context synthesis and diverse result selection.
Documents quantization options, distance metrics, cache settings, and performance tips.
Includes MCP setup guidance for using AgentDB tools in Claude Code.
limitations: Requires Node.js, AgentDB, and an embedding provider or custom embedding model.
Uses npx agentdb@latest examples, so users should pin versions before production use.
Does not provide a complete application, deployment plan, or access-control design.
Performance claims depend on data size, hardware, index settings, and embedding dimensions.

use_cases.title: Build a Knowledge Base Search
use_cases.description: Create semantic retrieval over internal documents with embeddings, metadata filters, and similarity thresholds.

use_cases.title: Prototype a RAG Pipeline
use_cases.description: Connect vector retrieval to answer generation with relevant context and diverse search results.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Vector Database Settings
use_cases.description: Compare distance metrics, quantization modes, and indexing settings for an AgentDB workload.

prompt_templates.title: Initialize a Vector Database
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AgentDB vector search skill to help me initialize a local vector database for 1536-dimensional OpenAI embeddings.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Document Search Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AgentDB vector search skill to design a document search flow with embeddings, metadata filters, and top-k retrieval.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Retrieval Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AgentDB vector search skill to tune similarity thresholds, distance metrics, and MMR settings for better retrieval quality.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Production RAG Backend
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AgentDB vector search skill to review my RAG backend plan, including data handling, version pinning, indexing, and MCP tool use.

output_examples.input: I need to search product documentation by meaning, not exact keywords.
output_examples.output: The skill would outline an AgentDB setup with document embeddings, metadata fields, similarity thresholds, and a query workflow.

output_examples.input: My vector search returns repetitive results.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend MMR, threshold tuning, metadata filters, and checks for embedding dimension consistency.

output_examples.input: I want to connect vector search to Claude Code tools.
output_examples.output: The skill would describe the AgentDB MCP setup and warn users to review package versions and tool permissions first.

best_practices: Pin AgentDB package versions before running CLI or MCP commands in shared environments.
Keep vector database files and exports out of source control when they contain private content.
Match embedding dimensions, distance metrics, and thresholds to the selected embedding model.
anti_patterns: Do not run @latest package commands in production without review and version pinning.
Do not store confidential documents or embeddings without retention and access-control rules.
Do not compare search quality before confirming embedding dimensions and distance metrics.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build AgentDB vector search workflows for semantic document retrieval, hybrid filtering, and RAG context lookup.

faq.question: Does it include runnable application code?
faq.answer: No. It provides commands, TypeScript patterns, tuning guidance, and troubleshooting steps.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is there a medium security rating?
faq.answer: The skill recommends npx package execution, MCP setup, and local database files. These are useful but need user review.

faq.question: Does the skill show credential exfiltration?
faq.answer: No evidence of credential exfiltration was found in SKILL.md.

faq.question: What should users check before using it?
faq.answer: Users should pin package versions, protect database files, and review MCP permissions before enabling tools.
